united-states-of-america: It spans more time zones than any other single country on Earth.
Roughly 40% of its land area drains into the Mississippi River system.
It is one of only three nations with a coastline on both the Atlantic and Pacific oceans.
Its national mammal, the bison, was nearly hunted to extinction in the 19th century.
More than half of its states have a smaller population than many world capitals alone.
china: It is home to more than 1,400 living languages and dialect groups.
The world's longest man-made waterway still runs through its eastern heartland.
It shares land borders with 14 countries — more than almost any nation.
Its territory includes the planet's highest plateau outside the Himalayas.
It produces more cement in two years than the United States did in the entire 20th century.
russia: It crosses 11 time zones — the most of any country.
Lake Baikal inside its borders holds more unfrozen freshwater than all North American lakes combined.
The Trans-Siberian railway crosses nearly 9,300 km of its territory.
It has both the world's largest forest area and extensive permafrost.
Its European and Asian halves are divided by the Ural Mountains.
brazil: It contains roughly 60% of the Amazon rainforest.
It is the only country in the Americas where Portuguese is the official language.
Its coastline stretches more than 7,400 km along the Atlantic.
It shares a border with every South American country except Chile and Ecuador.
It leads the world in biodiversity of primates and freshwater fish.
australia: It is the only country that is also a continent.
More than 80% of its mammals are found nowhere else on Earth.
The Great Barrier Reef off its northeast coast is visible from space.
Most of its population lives within 100 km of the coast.
Its banknotes are printed on polymer plastic, not cotton paper.
india: It has 22 officially recognized languages — the most of any nation.
The monsoon season delivers most of its annual rainfall in just four months.
It is the world's largest producer of milk.
Its railway network is one of the largest employers on the planet.
It shares the world's highest militarized border with one neighbor to the north.
canada: It has more lakes than the rest of the world combined.
Its coastline is the longest of any country.
It is the world's largest producer of maple syrup.
More than half its land is covered by boreal forest.
It uses six primary time zones across its width.
argentina: It is the world's largest Spanish-speaking country by area.
Patagonia in its south is home to glaciers that feed southern oceans.
It was among the first countries to adopt a national dinosaur heritage site.
The tango music tradition emerged in its port cities in the late 1800s.
It claims sovereignty over a slice of Antarctica larger than its mainland.
mexico: It introduced chocolate, chilies, and corn to the world through trade.
It sits on the Ring of Fire with more than 40 active volcanoes.
The monarch butterfly migrates thousands of miles to its central highlands.
It is the world's largest Spanish-speaking country by population.
Ancient civilizations here developed one of the earliest writing systems in the Americas.
france: It has the most time zones of any European country due to overseas territories.
Its high-speed rail network carries more passengers than many national airlines.
It produces more than 400 distinct cheese varieties.
The world's most visited art museum sits in its capital — but that's not today's clue.
It is the largest agricultural producer in the European Union.
germany: It has more than 1,500 different types of sausage.
Forests cover roughly one-third of its land area.
It is the world's third-largest exporter of goods.
The autobahn network famously has long stretches without a speed limit.
It shares borders with nine countries — the most of any EU member.
united-kingdom: It is made up of four distinct countries under one crown.
No point in it is more than about 120 km from the sea.
It was the first nation to industrialize at scale in the 18th century.
Its legal system split into separate traditions centuries ago in different regions.
It operates more offshore wind capacity than any other country.
italy: It has more UNESCO World Heritage sites than any other country.
It is the world's largest wine producer by volume.
Active volcanoes still shape life in parts of its south.
The boot-shaped peninsula extends deep into the Mediterranean.
It was a patchwork of city-states for centuries before unification in 1861.
spain: It produces nearly half the world's olive oil.
The siesta tradition reflects its hot inland summer climate.
It was a global empire whose currency circulated across four continents.
Flamenco music and dance emerged from its southern Andalusian region.
It shares a land border with only one other European country besides Portugal.
japan: It sits on the junction of four tectonic plates.
More than 70% of its land is mountainous and forested.
It has the world's highest density of vending machines per capita.
Shinkansen bullet trains have run with almost no fatal accidents for decades.
Its population has one of the highest life expectancies on Earth.
south-africa: It has three capital cities, each serving a different branch of government.
It is the only African nation in the G20.
The Cape floral region here has more plant species than most entire continents.
It mines a large share of the world's platinum and chromium.
Eleven official languages reflect its diverse population.
egypt: More than 95% of its people live along the Nile River corridor.
It controls the Suez Canal, a shortcut between two oceans.
Ancient papyrus from here was among humanity's first writing surfaces.
The Sahara covers most of its land, leaving a thin green strip along one river.
It is the most populous country in the Arab world.
nigeria: It is Africa's most populous country and has the largest economy on the continent.
Nollywood here produces more films per year than Hollywood.
The Niger River delta in its south is one of the world's largest wetlands.
It has over 500 indigenous languages still spoken today.
It is a major exporter of crude oil from its coastal shelf.
turkey: It straddles two continents separated by the Bosphorus strait.
It is the world's largest producer of hazelnuts.
The ancient city of Troy was excavated on its northwest coast.
It is home to more than 3 million refugees — among the highest globally.
Hot-air balloon tourism thrives over its central volcanic landscape.
indonesia: It is the world's largest archipelago nation with over 17,000 islands.
It is the most populous Muslim-majority country on Earth.
The Komodo dragon is found only on a few of its islands.
It sits on the Pacific Ring of Fire with frequent volcanic activity.
It was once the world's largest producer of nutmeg and cloves.
saudi-arabia: Rub' al Khali — the Empty Quarter — covers much of its south.
It holds roughly one-fifth of the world's proven oil reserves.
It has no permanent rivers — only seasonal wadis.
Islam's two holiest cities lie within its borders.
It is building a linear desert city planned to run 170 km long.
iran: It has never been colonized despite centuries of foreign influence.
Persian carpets from here have been traded along Silk Road routes for millennia.
It holds about 10% of the world's proven oil reserves.
The Zagros Mountains run along much of its western spine.
Nowruz, the Persian New Year, is celebrated by millions across the region.
pakistan: It is home to five of the world's fourteen highest peaks.
The Indus River civilization here dates back over 4,000 years.
It became the first Muslim-majority nation with a nuclear arsenal.
K2, the world's second-highest mountain, straddles its northern border.
It produces more than half the world's footballs by hand-stitching.
thailand: It is the only Southeast Asian nation never colonized by Europe.
Rice paddies cover much of its central floodplain.
It was known internationally as Siam until 1939.
Bangkok's canal network earned it the nickname 'Venice of the East' — but skip the capital.
It is the world's largest exporter of rice by value.
vietnam: It is the world's second-largest coffee exporter.
The Mekong Delta in its south is a maze of rivers and rice fields.
It has one of the fastest-growing economies in Southeast Asia.
Ha Long Bay's limestone karsts draw millions of visitors — not today's answer though.
Motorbikes outnumber cars in its major cities by a wide margin.
south-korea: It has the world's fastest average internet speeds.
K-pop and K-drama exports generate billions in cultural revenue.
More than 70% of its land is mountainous.
It transformed from war devastation to a high-tech economy in one generation.
Kimchi, fermented vegetables, is served with nearly every meal.
colombia: It is the world's leading exporter of emeralds.
It is one of only 17 megadiverse countries on Earth.
Coffee from its Andean slopes is prized worldwide.
It is the only South American country with coastlines on two oceans.
The Amazon rainforest covers roughly a third of its territory.
poland: It is the European Union's largest producer of apples.
Medieval salt mines here are UNESCO-listed underground cathedrals.
The Vistula River runs north through its heartland.
It regained independence in 1918 after more than a century of partitions.
Pierogi dumplings are a staple dish with dozens of regional fillings.
ukraine: It is often called the breadbasket of Europe for its fertile black soil.
Chernobyl's exclusion zone lies in its north.
It is the largest country entirely within Europe by area.
The Dnieper River divides much of its territory.
It was a founding republic of the Soviet Union in 1922.
sweden: It has more islands than any other country — over 260,000.
The Nobel Prizes in science and literature are awarded in its capital.
Forests cover about 70% of its land.
It has not fought a war in over two centuries.
The Northern Lights are visible from its far north in winter.
norway: Its fjords were carved by Ice Age glaciers.
It has the world's largest sovereign wealth fund from oil revenues.
The midnight sun shines for weeks in its Arctic north.
Salmon farming is a major export industry along its coast.
It shares the longest border in Europe with one western neighbor.
